## Summary
The w0-wa parametrization of the dark energy equation of state provides a flexible framework for testing whether dark energy evolves over time. Recent combined analyses of BAO, CMB, and Type Ia supernovae data show a ~2.5σ preference for evolving dark energy (w0 > -1, wa < 0), challenging the cosmological constant model.

## Evidence
DESI Year-1 BAO measurements combined with Planck CMB and DESY5 supernova data yield w0 = -0.55 ± 0.21 and wa = -1.27 (+0.40/-0.34). The statistical preference for dynamical dark energy over ΛCDM reaches 2.5σ when combining all probes, though individual datasets remain consistent with w = -1.

## Significance
If confirmed by DESI Year-3 and Euclid data, evolving dark energy would be the most significant discovery in cosmology since the acceleration itself. The w0-wa constraints directly inform theoretical models of quintessence, phantom energy, and modified gravity alternatives.
